channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: 'The prince of prints': Mzukisi Mbane on building a modern African brand rooted in heritage
item.description: Our guest in this edition represents creativity, identity and entrepreneurship. Mzukisi Mbane is the brains behind Imprint South Africa. He founded the brand after originally studying accounting and has now built a globally recognised label rooted in African heritage and futurism. He spoke to us in Entre Nous.
item.dc:creator: Delano D’SOUZA

item.title: Labour hopes for 'smooth transition' as Starmer rival wins critical election
item.description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has congratulated Labour's Andi Burnham's win in the by-election that took place in the constituency of Makerfield on June 17, while saying he would fight any leadership challenge he is presented with. Burnham is Starmer's main rival, with many in Labour hoping that the new MP will now challenge the PM. FRANCE 24's Benedicte Paviot tells us more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Israel steps up attacks in Lebanon amid fallout with US over Iran deal
item.description: Israel’s military said on June 19 its forces struck targets throughout southern Lebanon overnight as Hezbollah reported intense fighting in the area, threatening the nascent agreement between Iran and the United States to end their war. Israel and Hezbollah are not parties to the agreement, but Iran insists Israel must withdraw from south Lebanon. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky tells us more from Jerusalem.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'No Scotland, no party': Tartan army take over Boston for first world cup since 1998
item.description: Scotland fans have taken over Boston as they play their first World Cup since 1998, and are set to play Morocco in their second game of the tournament on June 19. The Tartan army has conquered the hearts of Bostonians, who will be sad to see them go: after their game against Morocco, the Scottish team will be flying to Miami to face Brazil. FRANCE 24's Selina Sykes meets the Tartan army ahead of the game.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Starmer's main rival wins key Makerfield election, looks to premiership
item.description: Labour's Andy Burnham has won a by-election in northern England, fuelling speculation that he could mount a challenge to Prime Minister Keir Starmer. Starmer’s popularity has cratered since he led the center-left Labour Party to a landslide election victory in July 2024. Labour is losing liberal voters to the growing Green Party, and facing a rising Reform UK, which consistently leads in nationwide opinion polls.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: US-Iran peace negotiations abruptly called off amid growing criticism
item.description: Talks ⁠that had ​been planned for ​June 19 between the United States and Iran ⁠in Switzerland were abruptly called off, as the Trump administration faces mounting criticism from both Democrats and Republicans. This comes after Iran delayed sending its delegation to Switzerland over Israel’s ongoing military campaign in Lebanon. FRANCE 24's Douglas Herbert.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Iran says Trump agreed to deal terms 'out of desperation' as US Democrats blast agreement
item.description: US Vice President JD Vance says he doesn’t know if he’ll be headed to Geneva on June 19 where he was due to attend a signing ceremony for an initial deal to end the war in Iran. But an agreement has already been signed by Donald Trump and Iranian officials, so Vance said that further negotiations could happen in Switzerland on the weekend. Meanwhile, Iran’s supreme leader has issued his first statement on the deal, saying that President Trump agreed to terms out of desperation.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Switzerland close in on qualification for round of 16
item.description: Switzerland cruised to a 4-1 win over Bosnia and Herzegovina to bounce back after their opening draw against Qatar. The Nati are now close to securing a place in the round of 16.
item.dc:creator: L'Equipe TV

item.title: Moroccan footballer Achraf Hakimi to be tried in France for rape, appeals court says
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Moroccan footballer Hakimi to be tried in France for rape, appeals court says
item.description: A French court confirmed Friday that Moroccan footballer Achraf Hakimi will stand trial for rape. The 27-year-old footballer, who plays for Paris Saint-Germain (PSG), denies the allegations and said he was "eagerly awaiting" his trial.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Iran to lodge complaint with FIFA over World Cup travel restrictions
item.description: The Iranian World Cup team will lodge a complaint with world football association FIFA over the unfair travel restrictions imposed on them during the North American tournament, Iran's football federation said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: 🔴Middle East live: US-Iran talks postponed as Israel carries out deadly strikes in southern Lebanon
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: US launches Germany pharma pricing probe, raising threat of new tariffs
item.description: Germany came under fresh US trade scrutiny on Thursday as the United States launched an investigation into the country's pharmaceutical pricing policies, a move that could pave the way for new tariffs. Washington said it would examine whether Germany's pricing practices unfairly underpay for innovative medicines and burden US commerce under Section 301 trade authorities.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Mexico becomes first country to reach knockout stage after beating South Korea
item.description: Co-hosts Mexico became the first team to secure a place in the World Cup knockout stage after a 1-0 victory over South Korea at a jubilant Guadalajara Stadium on Thursday. Luis Romo scored shortly after half-time, capitalising on a goalkeeping error, to seal Mexico's second straight win and confirm them as Group A winners with one match remaining.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup? Not today: New York is celebrating the Knicks
item.description: The FIFA World Cup may be under way in the United States, but in New York on Thursday, all eyes were on basketball. Tens of thousands of fans packed the streets of Manhattan to celebrate the NBA champion Knicks as the team paraded through the city under tight security. FRANCE 24’s Juliette Montilly was there.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Mexico become first team to reach World Cup knockout phase
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Starmer rival Burnham wins Makerfield as Labour leadership speculation grows
item.description: Labour's Andy Burnham won the Makerfield parliamentary by-election on Friday, defeating Reform UK candidate Rob Kenyon and returning to Westminster after years as Manchester mayor. The victory strengthens Burnham's position as a potential successor to Prime Minister Keir Starmer, as speculation grows over Labour's future leadership.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: EU leaders back stronger trade defences amid Chinese export surge
item.description: EU leaders agreed on Thursday to strengthen trade defences against a Chinese exports surge that Brussels views as a threat to European industry, while maintaining “constructive dialogue” with Beijing. The move reflects growing concern over the EU’s dependence on China, as the bloc’s goods trade deficit reached about 360 billion euros last year.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Middle East live: Israel pounds southern Lebanon, US-Iran talks postponed
item.description: Israeli strikes across southern Lebanon on Friday killed 18 people, according to Lebanese state media, while the Israeli military said four soldiers were killed, its first losses since a US-Iran interim deal, which calls for the cessation of hostilities in Lebanon, was signed. US-Iran talks scheduled for Friday in Switzerland have been postponed. Follow our liveblog for all the latest updates.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Colombia sees new peace breakthrough as 100 rebels hand over arms
item.description: About 100 guerrillas surrendered their weapons in southern Colombia on Thursday under a peace deal with leftist President Gustavo Petro. The surrender came days before Sunday's presidential runoff election between Petro ally Senator Ivan Cepeda, who promises to continue the peace initiative, and far-right candidate Abelardo de la Espriella, who opposes it.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Canada overpower nine-man Qatar in dominant display
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Canada overpower nine-man Qatar in dominant display
item.description: Canada secured their first-ever World Cup victory with a 6-0 rout of nine-man Qatar in a Group B match overshadowed by a serious injury to midfielder Ismael Koné and two red cards for Qatar.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Knicks fans flood New York for massive NBA Finals championship parade
item.description: Knicks fans packed Manhattan on Thursday for a heavily secured victory parade celebrating the New York team's NBA Finals triumph, turning the city into a sea of blue and orange. Fans chanted "Let's go Knicks" as the procession moved through packed streets, with some paying hundreds of dollars for line sitters to secure prime viewing spots overnight.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Canada - Qatar: Relive Les Rouges' first World Cup win in dominant display
item.description: Canada produced a dominant display to dismantle Qatar 6-0 in their Group B match on Thursday, taking full control after a chaotic second half in which the visitors collapsed both structurally and numerically. Follow the key moments from the match below.
item.dc:creator: Mehdi BOUZOUINA
